task: Built and deployed a four-stage permission gate system to prevent dangerous task injection in unrestricted sessions, added task approval workflow with notification integration, completed Phase 3.5 security hardening with full test coverage, cut first tagged release (v0.1.0) with Homebrew formula and GitHub release, and verified end-to-end installation via homebrew-askr tap.
discussion_summary: Askr has progressed through three major phases: Phase 3 (notifications) and Phase 3.5 (permission guard) are fully implemented and tested; Phase 4 (approval workflow) is wired into the Cursor extension. This session completed the v0.1.0 release cycle by computing the real tarball sha256, fixing the Homebrew formula to reference the correct GitHub release artifact, creating the homebrew-askr tap repository, and verifying end-to-end installation. Critical gap remains: guard_warning notification type is not wired into Cursor extension.js type whitelist, so Phase 3.5 IDE popups do not render despite being marked Done in roadmap. README.md still lists Phase 3 and 3.5 as 'Coming Next' and requires update to reflect completion.

accomplishments.what: LinkedIn location combobox field filling fixed with city name extraction and fallback retry pattern
Identified root cause of LinkedIn location field failures: full location strings do not trigger city autocomplete dropdown
Implemented two-part fix: prompt instructs extraction of city name from full location string, with retry on failure
Killed orphaned uvicorn process blocking backend logs
Conducted comprehensive security audit of apply agent code generation paths with four hardening fixes against prompt injection attacks
Fixed resume PDF portfolio URL lookup from qa_bank.portfolio_url to application_prefill.answers.portfolio_url
Updated PDF generator to render 'Portfolio' as link label instead of domain URL
Diagnosed Ramp application failure as Ashby spam_warning state (browser fingerprinting-based anti-bot detection)
Implemented spam_warning recovery with 'Learn more' probe to locate submit/submit-again button
Extended spam_warning handling to distinguish overlay banner (resubmit after scroll) vs form replacement (hard refresh required)
Refactored spam recovery strategy to defer spam-flagged jobs to end of session instead of inline retry
Investigated queue drain architecture and browser_stream replay buffer lifecycle
Implemented permission_gate.py to detect dangerous permissions (skip-permissions, unrestricted Bash, rm in allow list)
Implemented session_start.py to hold queued tasks instead of auto-injecting when dangerous permissions detected
Wired task_approval_pending notification type into Cursor extension.js for IDE popup rendering
Added test_permission_gate.py with 13 passing tests covering all dangerous permission detection scenarios
Added test_task_approval_gate.py with full coverage for approval gate workflow
Updated roadmap.md to reflect Phase 3.5 (guard) completion and Phase 4 (approval workflow) status
Auto-completed 'dangerously-skip-permissions sessions before queued' goal via Stop-hook inference
Computed real tarball sha256 for v0.1.0 release and updated Homebrew formula
Fixed Homebrew formula URL to reference v0.1.0 GitHub release artifact
Created homebrew-askr tap repository with Formula/askr.rb and pushed to GitHub
Fixed brew audit lint issues in formula test block
Verified end-to-end Homebrew installation: brew tap, brew install, and binary execution all successful
Logged v0.1.0 release and homebrew-askr tap decision to decisions.jsonl

next_actions.action: Wire guard_warning notification type into Cursor extension.js type whitelist (add to NOTIFICATION_TYPES or equivalent) so Phase 3.5 IDE popups actually render
next_actions.why: guard_warning is marked Done in roadmap but does not display in IDE because extension does not recognize the type; this is a critical gap blocking Phase 3.5 user-facing functionality

next_actions.action: Update README.md: change Phase 3 (notifications) and Phase 3.5 (guard) from 'Coming Next' to 'Completed' or 'In Production', and add v0.1.0 release notes with Homebrew installation instructions
next_actions.why: README is stale and misleads users about feature availability; v0.1.0 is now released and installable via Homebrew

next_actions.action: Extend pre_tool_use.py cross-repo boundary check to cover Bash tool calls, not just Write/Edit/MultiEdit operations
next_actions.why: currently only file edits are guarded against cross-repo violations; Bash commands can also escape the repository boundary and should be checked

next_actions.action: Fix PreCompact emergency handover to route through real LLM handover path instead of hardcoded boilerplate (checkpoint.py create_checkpoint, trigger_type==emergency branch)
next_actions.why: emergency handovers currently use static text instead of generating proper context; this reduces handover quality when sessions hit token limits

decisions.decision: Created separate homebrew-askr tap repository (BippinShekar/homebrew-askr) instead of submitting to Homebrew core
decisions.reason: Homebrew core requires stable upstream releases and extensive testing; a custom tap allows rapid iteration and updates without core review overhead

relational_files.why: core guard logic for detecting dangerous permissions; wired into session_start.py
calls permission_gate.py to hold queued tasks in dangerous sessions
13 passing tests covering all dangerous permission detection scenarios
full coverage for approval gate workflow
notification type whitelist must include guard_warning and task_approval_pending for IDE popups to render
tracks Phase 3.5 (guard) and Phase 4 (approval) completion status
needs update to reflect Phase 3 and 3.5 completion and v0.1.0 release

blockers: guard_warning notification type not wired into Cursor extension.js type whitelist — Phase 3.5 IDE popups do not render despite being marked Done
completed_goals: Wire task_approval_pending notification type into Cursor extension.js for IDE popup rendering - also audit whether guard_warning (Phase 3.5) actually renders, since it is not in the extension type whitelist either
